The '''''Star of Empire''''' was a [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury liner]] owned by [[Haj Shipping Lines]].

==Characteristics==
The ''Star of Empire'' was an oval [[Starship/Legends|starship]], nearly two [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] long, and painted bright blue with decorative lights. With forty decks, it had the capacity to carry five thousand passengers and was staffed by a large crew and droids, including [[crablike maintenance droid]]s.

It had an enormous hangar bay where shuttles or smaller ships owned by wealthy passengers, could dock. All kinds of ships were there: from fighters and pinnaces, two barges and skids, plus the service auto carts. It took half an hour to close and pressurize the bay. Space-suited crew received passengers; accommodations were welcomed and escorted to their staterooms, whereas the others, carrying their own luggage, were herded by [[purser droid]]s. The hangar did not have an [[Energy field/Legends|energy field]] but [[pressure door]]s operated by the Hangar Control; ships couldn't depart at any time, but pilots had to fill requests thirty [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]] earlier.

The ''Star of Empire'' had forty staterooms on either side of the hull, each having a large portal viewing into open space; suites were large and spacious, with vases decorated with [[Stasis/Legends|stasis]]-fresh flowers, well-secured crates of [[Carida/Legends|Caridian]] glassware, and Empire-style furniture.

Other places of interest included a low-paying passenger area called the "[[Starlight Deck]]" with no viewports, an [[Observation deck/Legends|observation deck]], [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] [[Casino/Legends|lounges]] such as the "[[Corellia Room]]," the expensive "[[Nebula Room]]" [[Restaurant/Legends|restaurant]], cheap [[cafeteria]]s for the more common passengers, and exercise rooms; there was also a small but extensive [[Library/Legends|library]] with millions of electronic books in thousands of [[Language/Legends|languages]] for remote access or download, as well as tutorials and [[virtual reality]] games; it had a raised area for readings or VR performances, and cubicles.<ref name="SFE">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref><ref name="SFEA">[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'' audio drama]]</ref>

The ''Star of Empire'' had a sister ship also produced by Haj Shipping Lines, named [[Queen of Empire]].

==History==
The ''Star'' served a cosmopolitan crowd of passengers, [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]], [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinians]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], [[Gran/Legends|Grans]]; merchants, engineers, technicians, bounty hunters, Imperials. The Haj family was generous and political enough to offer reduced fares to some Imperial military personnel, something appreciated by the senior officers.<ref name="SFE" /> On the other hand, Rebels might enjoy a more luxurious cabin, thanks to the sympathies of some crewmembers.<ref name="SFEA" />

Because of this traffic, the ''Star'' became a "crossroad" of businesses and criminal activities; the bounty hunter [[Slyder]] noticed it, and decided to stay onboard that ship, living in a cabin for years, spying on potential targets and selling their information (and later, their heads) to those interested to become his clients; he didn't need to look for new bounties or search [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for new targets. Slyder sometimes acquired the assistance of the [[porter droid]] [[NB4]]<ref name="SFEA" /> and during the years onboard the ''Star'' he had targeted assassin droids, con men, spies, robbers, Rebels.<ref name="SFE" />

In [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]], prior to possessing the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] was travellinga through [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] on the ''Star of Empire'' and other similar pleasure liners.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

As of [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], the ''Star'' was [[Captain/Legends|captained]] by one of the many granddaughters of [[Haj|Old Man Haj]]. That time Calrissian with [[Vuffi Raa]] was traveling again, and met [[Second Lieutenant/Legends|Second Lieutenant]] [[Kyle Katarn]] taking a pleasure cruise on the ''Star'' to relax after his graduation from the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]]; the family of [[Dol Donar II]] was also there. In the same time Rebel agents [[Jan Ors]], [[Ris Waller]] and [[Rosco Ross]], with [[A-Cee]], came with their ship ''[[Truly Sorry]]'' for a recruiting mission.<ref name="SFE" /><ref name="SFEA" />

The operations were detected by Slyder, as did Katarn's ambivalence, who decided to defect and join the Rebels. This information was sold to Governor Donar, who ordered their arrest. Although the Captain refused to "take sides", Imperial military personnel were recuited to assist Slyder. In the [[Skirmish on the Star of Empire|episodic skirmish]] that followed, the Rebels escaped on the ''Sorry'', not before shooting around the ship's hangar to force the Hangar Control to open the [[pressure door]] out of schedule.<ref name="SFE" /><ref name="SFEA" />

In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the ''Star'' was taken over by a rogue artificial intelligence created by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] called [[Systems Infiltration Manager|SIM]]. The anthropologist [[Mammon Hoole]], his wards [[Tash Arranda/Legends|Tash]] and [[Zak Arranda/Legends|Zak Arranda]], and the smuggler [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] escaped from the murderous program, and Dash disabled the cruiser with his ship, the ''[[Outrunner]]''. Dash planned to sell the ''Star of Empire'' to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].
